texts.text: the clock has forgotten which hour it was promising
a tide of photographs goes out and never returns
somewhere a projector dreams it is a lighthouse
the faces in the wallpaper agree to be patient
rust learns the shape of every hand that left
the moon is only a coin we keep losing on purpose
gardens close their eyes when no one is counting
machines hum the lullaby they were never taught
the sea keeps a museum of everything it swallowed
a door opens onto the inside of another morning
the staircase keeps a record of everyone who hesitated
we mistook the lighthouse for a question and answered anyway
every mirror in the house is rehearsing your absence
the orchard counts its ghosts by the weight of fallen fruit
a telegram arrives from a year that was never built
the snow keeps drafting letters it will never sign
somewhere the ocean is practicing your name in salt
the chandelier dreams in the dialect of drowned ships
all the trains agreed to arrive only in our sleep
the wallpaper memorizes us so the house can forget
a comet leaves its forwarding address in the dark
the field hums the frequency of everyone who walked away
the clocktower confesses it has been counting the wrong stars
rain rehearses the names of streets that drowned
the museum keeps one room for the weather we lost
every photograph is a window someone forgot to close
the moths are translating the lamp into something kinder
the garden left its gate open for a century, just in case
AND THEN THE LIGHT REMEMBERED US
A REEL WITH NO BEGINNING
A MEMORY YOU WERE NEVER ISSUED
THE PROJECTIONIST HAS FALLEN ASLEEP
EVERYTHING HERE IS ALREADY REMEMBERING YOU
INTERMISSION FOR THE TIDES
REEL TWO — THE HOUR WITHOUT A NUMBER
WE NOW RETURN YOU TO YOUR SLEEP
